docs.text: Brosio's Homelab Welcome to my homelab documentation. This site covers the infrastructure, services, experiments, and lessons behind my home lab. Project status: Anything marked WIP is planned or under construction. Everything else is active. Explore the Lab 🗺️ Architecture See how the network, infrastructure, and applications fit together. 🐧 Environment Explore the Proxmox virtualization platform powering the lab. 🤖 AI Local models, Ollama, OpenWebUI, and experimental AI projects. 🎮 Gaming Minecraft hosting, game automation, and save synchronization. 💾 File Management Media storage with Jellyfin and local file sharing with Samba. 📈 Monitoring Security logging, monitoring, and visibility across the lab. 🛜 Networking DNS, filtering, remote access, routing, VLANs, and subnets. 💻 Hardware The compact Lenovo ThinkCentre at the center of the setup. 🧠 Lessons Learned Notes and takeaways collected while building and maintaining the lab. 💭 About Why this homelab exists and what I hope to learn from it.
docs.title: Home

docs.text: 🗺️ Architecture See how the network, infrastructure, and applications fit together. 🐧 Environment Explore the Proxmox virtualization platform powering the lab. 🤖 AI Local models, Ollama, OpenWebUI, and experimental AI projects. 🎮 Gaming Minecraft hosting, game automation, and save synchronization. 💾 File Management Media storage with Jellyfin and local file sharing with Samba. 📈 Monitoring Security logging, monitoring, and visibility across the lab. 🛜 Networking DNS, filtering, remote access, routing, VLANs, and subnets. 💻 Hardware The compact Lenovo ThinkCentre at the center of the setup. 🧠 Lessons Learned Notes and takeaways collected while building and maintaining the lab. 💭 About Why this homelab exists and what I hope to learn from it.
docs.title: Explore the Lab

docs.text: This homelab allows me to tinker and experiment with a wide plethora of technologies that I find interesting. It continues to teach me invaluable skills that are best learned by getting my hands dirty— debugging by digging deep into config files, sifting through pages of documentation, forums, and half-hallucinated ChatGPT responses, and most importantly: persistence. I hope that this documentation will be useful to others who are interested in pursuing their own homelabs, and stands as a testament to my own learning journey. Enjoy, Brosio :)
docs.title: About

docs.text: crafty controller - craftycontroller.home.arpa used to manage a minecraft 26.1.2 server can handle 10 concurrent players plugins: GeyserMC - allows players to play on the same server with different versions of the game (Java/Bedrock) PaperMC - enables a more stable and secure Minecraft server epic games automatically claims Epic's weekly free games WIP local game sync DIY project to sync game saves across multiple devices on a local network intended for games with simple save files, such as emulated games, minecraft worlds, etc WIP ![[Pasted image 20260608012243.png]]

docs.text: AdGuard Home - adguard.home.arpa used to block unnecessary telemetry intrusive ads (especially on devices where a traditional adblocker cannot be installed) untrustworthy websites Caddy routes all incoming traffic to either AdGuard Home Dnsmasq implemented at the request of my girlfriend, who did not appreciate AdGuard's tendency to break her favorite websites Dnsmasq handles DNS resolution Tailscale used for secure remote access to the local network only trusted devices are allowed in the Tailnet VLANs and Subnets WIP
